decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader's thermal conductivity to enhance lateral heat spreading away from the localized heat source. I will apply a conservative 1.5x multiplier to the current spreader conductivity (453.6 -> 680.4), which remains within the allowed single-step ratio bounds and the absolute upper bound of 500.0 is not violated by this specific step logic (wait, 680.4 > 500.0). Correction: The bounds are [20.0, 500.0]. The current value is 453.6. A 1.5x increase would be 680.4, which violates the hard bound of 500.0. Therefore, I must cap the increase at the maximum allowed value of 500.0. This is a valid move within the [0.5x, 2.0x] step constraint (500/453.6 ≈ 1.1) and respects the absolute bound.

changes.reason: Increase spreader conductivity to the maximum allowed bound (500.0) to maximize lateral heat spreading and reduce the chip hot spot temperature. This step ratio (~1.1x) is within the permitted [0.5x, 2.0x] range.

expected_effects: Reduced thermal resistance in the spreader layer
Lower peak temperature in the chip component
Improved feasibility regarding the chip_max_temperature constraint
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur as conductivity approaches the upper bound
If this change is insufficient, geometric modifications (widening the spreader) will be required in the next iteration
